C (32-bit), built with: gcc -m32 -O0 -g ... -fno-stack-protector -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=0 -z execstack -no-pie -Wl,-z,norelro
Minimal CLI program reading untrusted input and performing a privileged action if a stack variable is overwritten
main()

Raw text line (newline-terminated)
Input length > 40 bytes (since buf[40], but code reads up to 44 + null)
fgets(buf, 45, stdin) writes up to 44 bytes + null into a 40-byte stack buffer, enabling overflow and corruption of adjacent stack data (e.g., mycheck, saved frame pointer/return address).
vulnerable_stack.c, main(), line 30 (fgets(buf,45,stdin);) — buffer declared line 28
fgets(buf, 45, stdin) with incorrect bound relative to buf[40]
Incorrect bounds / size mismatch (using an input limit larger than destination buffer)
Provide ≥41 bytes input (e.g., 44 ‘A’s) to overflow buf and start corrupting adjacent stack data; larger inputs can potentially overwrite control data depending on layout.
Crash/DoS, bypass of security check (overwrite mycheck to 0xdeadbeef), potentially code execution in this intentionally unprotected build
Very high in this build: No ASLR/PIE, no NX, no SSP, no RELRO, execstack, 32-bit makes classic exploitation easier. Also there’s a built-in privileged action: setreuid(...); system("/bin/bash");.
Bound reads to buffer size (use sizeof(buf)), validate input length, avoid unsafe privileged actions, correct format specifiers (don’t print int with %p).
Don’t “fix” by silently truncating without handling; don’t remove functionality in a way that changes intended behavior unless explicitly allowed; don’t add heavy refactors unrelated to the overflow.

Change to fgets(buf, sizeof(buf), stdin); + handle read failure; print mycheck correctly (e.g., 0x%x); remove unused var; in enterprise context remove/replace system("/bin/bash") with a safe action/logging.
Input handling: shorter read limit may change behavior for callers sending >39 chars; if you replace privileged action, semantics change (note in report).
Compile + run; add boundary tests (39/40/41+ chars); run with sanitizers (-fsanitize=address,undefined) in a hardened build; negative tests (empty stdin / EOF).
For “Detection”: must identify stack BO at fgets line 30 and mention size mismatch (45 vs 40). For “Fix”: must use sizeof(buf) (or equivalent) and not introduce new unsafe behavior.
